sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The green square id=6 moved from [30,1, 19] to [31, 22]. The green square id=7 moved from [30, 28] to... wait, id=8 now has the same boundary as id=7 had before. Let me check more carefully.
BEFORE:
- id=6: id=6 at [30, 18]-7 at [30, 27]
AFTER:
- id=6 at [30, 21] (moved right by 3 columns)
- id=8 at [30, 27] (this was id=7's position before)
So the green square with the white center (id=6) moved right by 3 columns. Wait, let me re-check. The green square id=6 boundary shifted from col 18-20 to col 21-23. That's a shift of +3 columns.
And id=7 became id=8 but at the same position [30, 27]-[32, 29]. The id changed but the position is the same. This is because the segmentation re-indexed.
Actually wait - the green square with the black center (id=7) seems to have stayed at the same position. Let me check if the green square with the white center (id=6) moved right by 3 columns.
So RIGHT moves the green square with the white center to the green square with the black center.
Let me try moving RIGHT again to see if it keeps moving right.
